name: Loanstar Title Loans
description_short: At 3201 Denton Hwy in Haltom City, TX, Loanstar Title Loans can be reached at 817-759-0700.
description_long:
LoanStar Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, GA, and has grown into a multi-location title loan company operating across Texas. With over three decades in the industry, the company positions itself as a long-standing provider helping working Americans access cash by leveraging the equity in their paid-off vehicles. Their Texas footprint includes multiple Fort Worth-area locations as well as stores in Haltom City, Richland Hills, and Burleson.

LoanStar offers car title loans and auto title loans up to $10,000. Borrowers must bring their vehicle, a government-issued photo ID, and a clear vehicle title — meaning no existing liens on the car. The company provides both single-installment and multiple-installment loan structures, with fee schedules publicly disclosed on their website for loan amounts of $600, $1,200, and $2,000. Payments can be made in cash, by check, debit card, or Western Union, and online payment is also available.

What sets LoanStar apart from smaller independent title lenders is their multi-location store network and their longevity in the market. They are regulated under the Texas OCCC (Office of Consumer Credit Commissioner) and publish required fee disclosures for both loan structures. Saturday hours (9AM–2PM) and weekday availability (10AM–6PM Monday–Friday) provide some scheduling flexibility, though they are closed Sundays.

Title loans are high-cost, collateral-based credit products — your vehicle secures the loan, and failure to repay can result in repossession. LoanStar's location page does not display APR figures prominently; borrowers must review the linked fee schedule disclosures to understand true cost. This product is best suited for vehicle owners who have exhausted lower-cost options and need cash quickly, not as a routine financial tool. The $10,000 ceiling is higher than most payday lenders but the collateral risk is significant.
pros: Loans up to $10,000 — higher ceiling than most payday or emergency cash lenders
Founded 1990 — 35+ years of operating history in the title loan industry
Multiple Fort Worth-area locations for in-person service
Accepts four payment methods: cash, check, debit card, and Western Union
Saturday hours (9AM–2PM) available at most locations
Fee schedule disclosures publicly linked on the website for $600, $1,200, and $2,000 loan amounts
OCCC-regulated with required Texas consumer credit notices posted
cons: Vehicle is collateral — non-payment can result in repossession of your car
Requires a clear title with no existing liens, excluding many potential borrowers
APR not displayed on the location page — borrowers must hunt for fee schedule PDFs
Texas locations only based on available information
Closed Sundays; weekday hours start at 10AM, limiting access for 9-to-5 workers
best_for: Vehicle owners with a paid-off car and a clear title needing $500–$10,000 quickly
Texas residents near a LoanStar location who prefer in-person loan processing
Borrowers who have been declined for personal loans due to poor or thin credit
People who can repay within the loan term and understand the collateral risk
diagnosis: LoanStar Title Loans is best for Texas vehicle owners with a clear title who need fast access to $500–$10,000 and have limited credit options. The main caveat is significant: your car backs the loan, and repossession is a real consequence of default — review the fee schedule disclosures carefully before borrowing.
services: Multi-location store network across North Texas
